key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 formed a denser, more protective oxide layer than DS, giving markedly better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal-storage containers, HVAF appears to be the more reliable coating route for extending high-temperature service life in nitrate-salt environments.
method: Tribaloy-400 coatings were deposited on ASTM A516 steel by HVAF and detonation spraying.
Specimens were exposed to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h.
Microstructure, phase formation, elemental mapping, mass gain, and spallation were compared.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O-rich surface with minimal salt penetration, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation after long exposure.
watch_item: The comparison is based on a specific salt composition and temperature, so performance should be revalidated for other CSP operating conditions and thermal cycling histories.
paper_title: Hot corrosion behavior of thermal sprayed using HVAF and DS techniques with Tribaloy-400 coatings for solar energy storage container
